key.name: monitors
key.description: The monitors whose brightness should be adjusted.

key.name: builtin-monitor
key.description: The name of the built-in monitor.

key.name: prevent-unredirect
key.description: If set to never, unredirection is never prevented. If set to when-correcting, unredirection will be prevented when the brightness is not at the maximum setting (and an alpha layer is lowering the brightness). If set to always, window unredirection will always be prevented when this extension is active, allowing tear-free display.

key.name: clone-mouse
key.description: When enabled, the mouse cursor follows the brightness setting. When disabled, the mouse cursor always remains at full brightness. Controlling mouse cursor brightness can sometimes show the wrong cursor and introduce cursor lag. You may want to disable it if you encounter cursor issues. Note that when other Gnome Shell components clone the mouse (like the Zoom accessibility feature), this feature may not work correctly, or there may be multiple cursors shown on the screen.

key.name: brightness-up
key.description: Keyboard shortcut to increase the overlay brightness by one step (5%). Set to an empty list to disable.

key.name: brightness-down
key.description: Keyboard shortcut to decrease the overlay brightness by one step (5%). Set to an empty list to disable.

key.name: dimming-mode
key.description: Controls how the brightness reduction is applied. "overlay" (default): a transparent black actor is placed over the screen — same behaviour as the original extension. "effect": applies a Clutter BrightnessContrastEffect to the stage. No overlay actor; near-darks clamp to black. "shader": applies a GLSL gamma-curve OffscreenEffect to the stage. Highlights are compressed faster than darks — white dims more than near-black at the same slider value. See shader-gamma to tune the curve shape.

key.name: shader-gamma
key.summary: Shader gamma exponent (shader dimming mode only).
key.description: Controls the gamma curve used in dimming-mode=shader. 1.0 gives the same linear result as the overlay mode. Values above 1.0 preserve dark content better while compressing highlights more aggressively — 2.0 is the recommended starting point.

key.name: per-workspace-brightness
key.description: When enabled, the extension remembers a separate brightness level for each workspace and restores it automatically when you switch. Set brightness on each workspace once; switching will apply that workspace's saved level thereafter.
